rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Assumptions and guarantees referencing component ports must be in component type scopes; implementation-level bindings go in implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structural integrity when inserting AGREE annexes; ensure all referenced identifiers exist in the model.
Use only valid AADL component names, ports, and data types in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts should declare assumptions and guarantees; implementation contracts should bind values to variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
Do not reference AADL properties like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing tokens such as ms/us/Hz/Bytes
Boolean data range comparisons with opaque data ports
Type mismatch diagnostics for assumed vs actual types
